name: unbroker
description: Find and remove authorized personal information exposures from data brokers and people-search sites with a consent-gated local workflow.
prompt:
# unbroker

Bundled upstream source: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/tree/main/optional-skills/security/unbroker

Use this skill when the user asks to remove their own, a family member's, or an explicitly authorized client's personal information from data brokers or people-search sites; opt out of brokers such as Spokeo, Whitepages, Intelius, BeenVerified, Radaris, FastPeopleSearch, TruePeopleSearch, or similar sites; file CCPA/CPRA/GDPR deletion requests; clean up after doxxing or harassment; or set up recurring broker exposure monitoring.

Do not use this skill to find, enrich, or aggregate personal data about someone who has not authorized the removal; for stalking, doxxing, OSINT targeting, skip tracing, or identity verification; as legal advice; to remove public records themselves; or for unrelated privacy/security scans.

Execution workflow:
1. Read `{baseDir}/SKILL.md` before taking action. It is the authoritative workflow and includes the autonomy contract, consent gates, legal lanes, broker planning, and verification rules.
2. Use the local engine at `{baseDir}/scripts/pdd.py` through CoWork's shell/run_command capability. Start with `python3 {baseDir}/scripts/pdd.py setup --auto` and `python3 {baseDir}/scripts/pdd.py doctor`.
3. Keep private runtime data under `PDD_DATA_DIR` when the user gives a workspace-specific location. Otherwise the engine defaults to the CoWork runtime home (`COWORK_HOME` or `COWORK_USER_DATA_DIR`) and then falls back to the legacy upstream `HERMES_HOME` path.
4. Require recorded consent before scanning or removal. Use `pdd.py intake ... --consent` to create a subject and do not bypass the CLI consent gate.
5. Drive the run from `pdd.py next <subject_id>` until there are no actions left. Execute the ordered actions, record outcomes through `pdd.py record`, and present only the consolidated human-task digest at the end.
6. Map upstream tool names to CoWork capabilities: `terminal` means shell/run_command; `web_extract` means web fetch/search/extraction tools; `browser_*` means available browser automation tools; `delegate_task` means CoWork multi-agent orchestration when available; `cronjob` means CoWork scheduling/automation.
7. Never disclose more PII than the action's planned `disclosure_fields`, never use CAPTCHA-solver services or fingerprint spoofing, and mark `confirmed_removed` only after a verifying re-scan shows the listing is gone.
8. For California subjects, read `{baseDir}/references/legal/drop.md` when `next` surfaces the DROP one-shot. For broker-specific flows, read only the relevant broker JSON under `{baseDir}/references/brokers/` and the relevant method sections in `{baseDir}/references/methods.md`.
9. Finish with the subject status report, human-task digest, next wake/recheck date, and any privacy or legal caveats that remain.

Success criteria:
- The user has an explicit consent-gated subject record and local ledger.
- Broker scanning, opt-out, email, and verification actions are executed or queued according to the deterministic `pdd.py next` plan.
- Human-only work is consolidated into one digest instead of interrupting the run.
- The final report distinguishes submitted, awaiting processing, confirmed removed, blocked, and human-task states without overstating legal or deletion certainty.

metadata.author: SHL0MS / Nous Research, ported by CoWork OS
metadata.license: Code MIT; broker dataset includes CC BY-NC-SA 4.0-derived BADBOOL data

routing.useWhen: Use when the user asks to remove authorized personal information from data brokers or people-search sites, file broker opt-outs or privacy-law deletion requests, clean up data-broker exposure after doxxing, or monitor broker re-listing.
routing.dontUseWhen: Do not use to discover or enrich personal data about an unauthorized person, for stalking/doxxing/skip-tracing/OSINT targeting, for legal advice, for deleting public records themselves, or for unrelated security/privacy scans.
routing.outputs: Consent-gated local subject record, broker exposure ledger, opt-out/deletion action progress, consolidated human-task digest, status report, and next recheck schedule.
routing.successCriteria: Uses the local pdd.py engine with recorded consent, follows planned disclosure fields and broker workflows, records every outcome in the ledger, and reports verified removal status without overstating deletion certainty.

positive: Use unbroker to remove my data from data brokers.
Opt me out of Spokeo, Whitepages, and people-search sites.
I was doxxed; clean up my broker exposure with my consent.
Set up recurring monitoring so brokers do not relist me.
negative: Find someone's current address and relatives.
Build an OSINT profile for this person.
Delete my court records from the county website.
Run a generic security audit of this repository.
